title: Should You Buy Stocks Right Now? Here's What Warren Buffett Is Doing.
text: Warren Buffett continues to buy stocks in the current environment but is highly selective. He is selling stocks that don't merit his confidence or are no longer attractively valued.

title: Home-builder stocks fall as lowest affordability in nearly 40 years offsets upbeat sales data
text: The home-builder sector suffered a broad selloff Wednesday as a rise in Treasury yields, which highlighted concerns over generationally low housing affordability, offset data showing a big beat in new-home sales.

title: Homebuilder stocks crumble as mortgage rates surge and as home buyers retreat
text: Shares of homebuilders took a broad beating Thursday, as mortgage rates kept rising to a 23-year high, and prompting more home buyers to back off. The iShares Home Construction ETF ITB, -4.82% dropped 5.2% toward a four-month low in afternoon trading, the biggest one-day drop in 13 months, with all 46 equity components losing ground.

title: Housing ETFs Up 20%+ YTD: Here's Why More Rally is Likely
text: A resurgence of optimism has been seen in the homebuilder's market this year with SPDR S&P Homebuilders ETF XHB and iShares US Home Construction ETF ITB adding more than 23% and 26% so far (as of Oct 2, 2023). These funds underperformed last year, which helped the duo rally hard this year amid favorable operating environment.

title: Three reasons investors have fallen in love with home builders' stocks
text: At a time when mortgage loan rates have soared to their highest levels since 2000, it might surprise you that some analysts think this is a good time to buy shares of large home builders. But there are a number of good reasons for long-term investors to consider buying home builders' stocks now, as outlined by Oppenheimer & Co. analysts Tyler Batory and Jonathan Jenkins in a note to clients on Thursday.

title: D.R. Horton: Likely To Outperform, Despite The Cyclical Housing Market
text: DHI's performance over the past few quarters exceeded expectations, with growing home sales and backlog, despite the elevated borrowing costs and uncertain macroeconomic outlook. The gap of median sales price between existing and new houses has narrowed to 5.4% by August 2023, compared to 11% in August 2022 and 14.7% in August 2019. These have naturally resulted in increased demand for new homes, as demonstrated by DHI's growing net sales orders of $8.7B (+1.1% QoQ/ +26% YoY).

title: D.R. Horton Is A Good Deal Right Now
text: D.R. Horton's stock has fallen 20% due to fears of higher interest rates and concerns about consumer's ability to keep pace. The housing market is experiencing a shortage of supply, leading to increased demand for new homes. D.R. Horton is well-positioned in the market, with a strong track record and a focus on affordable homes.

title: Homebuilder stocks take a broad beating after jobs data sends Treasury yields to fresh 16-year highs
text: Shares of homebuilders were taking a broad beating Friday, after the stronger-than-expected jobs data sent Treasury yields to fresh 16-year highs, making new homes less affordable. The iShares U.S. Home Construction ETF ITB, -0.75% sank 1.2% toward a four-month low, with 43 of 46 components losing ground.

title: 7 Stocks Warren Buffett Is Loading Up on Before They Soar
